decisions.description: Migrate all seven in-production plugins from the deprecated v1 PluginManager auto-registration to v2 Module classes using the @Module decorator with explicit wiring.
Replace TransactionBaseService inheritance with AbstractModuleService and use the model.define utility to get CRUD operations automatically from defined models.
Eliminate direct imports of core services across module boundaries; resolve dependencies through the Modules registry by name instead.
Convert plugin subscribers (the subscribers/ directory auto-registration pattern) to workflow steps using createWorkflow and createStep, which provide retry and compensation built in.

Per-plugin migration steps: convert the service to AbstractModuleService, convert subscribers to workflows, convert API routes to the new handler pattern, move config from plugins to modules in the config file, and regenerate migrations.
Audit each plugin's dependencies on core services before starting the migration and map them to the v2 Modules registry equivalents.
Preserve backward compatibility by running v1 and v2 API routes in parallel for one release cycle using the middlewares.ts pattern to register the legacy routes alongside the new ones.
The search plugin continues to wrap AbstractSearchService, which becomes a module type in v2 but retains the same createIndex / addDocuments / search method surface.

action_items.text: Migrate the fulfillment and inventory plugins (most complex)
Migrate the notification and webhook plugins
Migrate the pricing and loyalty plugins
Each owner delivers first two migrations with integration tests passing by end of sprint; check-in Wednesday
